title: Add autonomous spatial camera motion
summary: Create a seeded, autonomous camera motion system that smoothly drifts the perspective camera through 3D space, revealing parallax and depth structure. Camera motion is driven by layered sine harmonics (matching the evolution.ts pattern), modulated by bass energy and motionAmplitude, and entirely independent of pointer input.

files_to_create.purpose: Self-contained camera motion module with seeded harmonic orbits
files_to_create.details: Exports initCameraMotion(seed) which builds cached harmonic curves (reusing the evolution.ts pattern with createPRNG), and updateCamera(camera, elapsedMs, params) which evaluates those curves each frame to produce smooth position offsets around a base position. Parameters: 3-axis position harmonics with incommensurate periods (30-120s range), subtle rotation harmonics for look-target drift. Bass energy widens orbit radius. motionAmplitude scales all displacement (supports prefers-reduced-motion). Pointer has NO effect on camera (per AC). Camera always looks toward a slowly-drifting target point near origin.

files_to_modify.changes: Import and call updateCamera() each frame after evolveParams() and before renderer.render(). Pass elapsed time, bass energy, and motionAmplitude from the current VisualParams. Initialize camera motion on first real frame using the session seed.

details: Define CameraHarmonics interface: arrays of {amplitude, frequency, phase} for x, y, z position and lookTarget x, y, z

buildCameraHarmonics(seed): use createPRNG(seed + ':camera') to generate 2-3 harmonics per axis with incommensurate periods (30-120s), small amplitudes (0.3-1.5 units for position, 0.1-0.5 for look target)
Cache harmonics per seed (same pattern as evolution.ts curve cache)
evalAxis(harmonics[], elapsedMs): sum sin(elapsed * freq + phase) * amplitude
Export initCameraMotion(seed: string): void — builds and caches harmonics

Export updateCamera(camera: PerspectiveCamera, elapsedMs: number, bassEnergy: number, motionAmplitude: number): void — evaluates harmonics, applies bass modulation (1 + bassEnergy * 0.15) to position radius, scales all offsets by motionAmplitude, sets camera.position and camera.lookAt
Import initCameraMotion and updateCamera from cameraMotion.ts
In the geometry initialization block (first real frame), call initCameraMotion(deps.seed)
Each frame after evolveParams and before renderer.render: call updateCamera(camera, elapsed, smoothBass, currentParams.motionAmplitude)
No changes to pointer handling — pointer continues to affect only geometry disturbance
Visually confirm: camera drifts smoothly, parallax visible across point cloud depth layers
Confirm pointer movement does NOT orbit camera
Confirm reduced-motion preference dampens camera movement
Confirm bass pulses subtly widen camera orbit

design_decisions.decision: Seeded layered sine harmonics for camera path
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the proven evolution.ts pattern already in the codebase. Incommensurate frequencies prevent visible repetition. Deterministic per session seed.

design_decisions.decision: Camera orbits around a drifting target near origin rather than following a fixed path
design_decisions.rationale: The point cloud geometry is centered near origin. A slowly-drifting lookAt target adds organic feel while keeping the subject in view.

design_decisions.decision: Bass modulates orbit radius, not speed
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with mapping guidance (bass → macro motion). Radius modulation creates a subtle push/pull parallax effect on bass hits.
Ticket explicitly states OrbitControls may be used for dev but not as default interaction. Camera is fully autonomous.

design_decisions.decision: Base camera position stays near (0, 0, 5) with offsets of ±1-1.5 units
design_decisions.rationale: The geometry systems generate content roughly within a 3-unit radius of origin. Small camera offsets reveal parallax without losing the subject.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.Camera position changes over time: updateCamera() applies seeded harmonic offsets each frame, continuously moving camera position
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Camera movement reveals parallax across: 3-axis position drift with different phase/frequency per axis creates diagonal paths that reveal depth layers in the volumetric point cloud
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Movement is smooth and seeded: Sine harmonics are inherently smooth; all frequencies, amplitudes, and phases are derived from session seed via PRNG
acceptance_criteria_mapping.User pointer does not directly control o: Pointer state is not read or used in cameraMotion.ts; pointer only affects geometry disturbance via existing mappings.ts path

risks.risk: Camera drift moves too far, losing sight of geometry
risks.mitigation: Clamp total position offset magnitude; keep amplitudes conservative (tested with all 3 geometry modes)
motionAmplitude scaling (0.2 for reduced-motion users); slow harmonic periods (30-120s); no sudden acceleration
